Oracc: HBTIN — Hellenistic Babylonia: Texts, Iconography, Names

YOS 20, 090 [Sale of property][via hbtin]

Obverse
oo beginning broken
o 1'1'

[...] ⸢x⸣ [...]

(o 1') [...]

o 2'2'

⸢ša₂⸣ mni-din-⸢tu₄⸣-[d60 DUMU ša₂ md60]-⸢ŠEŠ-MU⸣nu lu₂?[...]

(o 2') of Nidintu-Anu, son of Anu-ah-ittannu [...] and the dead-end street; 34 cubits, the lower length [to the south ...] adjacent to the house of [...]-iddin, son of Nidintu-Anu; 10[+n cubits], the upper width to the west, adjacent to the house of [...], son of Ištar-ahhe-iddin, and the dead-end street [...], the lower width to the east, adjacent to the [narrow] street, "Passageway of the People." The total measurements: 34 cubits in lengths and [... cubits in widths] for that house; that house, as little and as much exist, all of it, belongs to Nidintu-Anu, son of Anu-ah-ittannu, son of Iqiša.

o 3'3'

u SILA la ⸢a⸣-[ṣu-u₂] ⸢34⸣ KUŠ₃ ⸢US₂⸣ KI⸢u₂⸣ [tu₁₅U₁₈.LU ...]

o 4'4'

DA E₂ m⸢x-x⸣-MU DUMU ša₂ mni-din-tu₄-d60 10+[x KUŠ₃]

o 5'5'

SAG.KI AN.⸢TA tu₁₅⸣MAR.TU DA E₂ m⸢x⸣-[...]

o 6'6'

DUMU ša₂ mdINANA-ŠEŠ-MEŠ-MU u SILA la a-ṣu-u₂ [...]

o 7'7'

SAG.KI KI.TA tu₁₅KUR.RA DA SILA [qat₂-nu]

o 8'8'

mu-⸢taq⸣ UN-MEŠ ŠU.NIGIN 34 KUŠ₃ US₂ ⸢u⸣ [... KUŠ₃ SAG.KI]

o 9'9'

mi-⸢šiḫ⸣-ti₃ E₂ MU-MEŠ E₂ MU-MEŠ ⸢i-ṣi⸣

o 1010

u ma-a-du ⸢ma⸣-la ba-šu-u₂ gab-bi ša₂ mni-din-⸢tu₄⸣-[d]⸢60⸣

o 11'11'

DUMU ša₂ md60-ŠEŠ-MUnu A m i!(GAN.)BA-⸢a⸣

o 12'12'

ki-i 2 MA.⸢NA KU₃.BABBAR⸣ qa-lu-u₂ md60-ŠEŠ-⸢MEŠ-MU DUMU⸣ [ša₂]

(o 12') If Anu-ahhe-iddin, son of Anu-mukin-apli, descendant of Sin-leqi-unninni, with Nidintu-Anu, son of Anu-ah-ittannu, son of Iqiša, declare that two mina of refined silver is the price, he bought it.

o 13'13'

md60-DU-A A md30-[TI]-⸢ER₂ KI mni-din⸣-[tu₄-d60]

o 14'14'

DUMU ša₂ md60-ŠEŠ-MUnu A m [i]BA-a ⸢GANBA⸣ [im-be₂-e-ma]

o 15'15'

i-šam ŠAM₂ E₂-šu₂ ⸢MU⸣-MEŠ TIL-⸢MEŠ u₃⸣ [2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR]

(o 15') The complete price of that house and [the two shekels of] additional [silver], he paid.

o 16'16'

a-tar SUM-su ŠU.NIGIN 2 ⸢MA.NA⸣ u₃ 2 ⸢GIN₂⸣ [KU₃.BABBAR a-di 2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR]

(o 16') The total, the two mina and two shekels [of silver, including the two shekels of silver] which was in accordance with the additional amount, he paid.

o 17'17'

⸢ša₂ a⸣-ki-i KA a-tar SUM⸢na⸣ KU₃.BABBAR ⸢a₄⸣ [2 MA.NA 2] ⸢GIN₂⸣

(o 17') That silver, the [two mina, two] shekels of refined silver, the [complete] price of silver of that house, [Ni]dintu-Anu, son of [Anu]-ah-[ittannu], received [from Anu-ahhe-iddin, son of] Anu-mukin-apli, as payment.

o 18'18'

[qa]-lu-u₂ ŠAM₂ E₂-šu₂ ⸢ka⸣-[sap TIL-MEŠ]

o 19'19'

[mni]-⸢din⸣-tu₄-d60 DUMU ša₂ m⸢d⸣[60]-⸢ŠEŠ⸣-[MUnu ina ŠU-MIN md60-ŠEŠ-MEŠ-MU]

o 20'20'

[DUMU ša₂ md]⸢60-DU⸣-A ma-ḫir ⸢a-pil⸣ ru-gum₂-⸢ma ul⸣ [i-ši]

(o 20') He shall not [have] a claim. They shall not return and raise a claim against one another.

o 21'21'

[ul i-tur]-⸢ra-ma⸣ <KI> a-ḫa-miš ul i-rag-⸢gu⸣-[mu]

Reverse
r 1r 1

[ma-ti-ma] ⸢ma⸣-na-⸢ma⸣ ša₂ ⸢E₁₁-ma⸣ UGU E₂ MU-MEŠ

(r 1) Anyone who [ever] comes about and raises a claim on that house, saying, "He did not sell that house; the silver was not received," the claimaint shall pay the silver (which) he received up to twelve times over.

r 22

[i-rag]-⸢gu⸣-mu ⸢um⸣-ma ⸢E₂ MU⸣-MEŠ ul na-din-ma

r 33

⸢KU₃⸣.[BABBAR] ⸢ul ma-ḫir⸣ i-⸢qab⸣-[bu]-⸢šu₂ lu₂⸣pa-qir-a-ni

r 44

⸢KU₃⸣.[BABBAR im]-⸢ḫur⸣ a-di 12-⸢TA⸣-[AM₃ i]-⸢ta-nap-pal⸣

r 55

[ina] ⸢ka⸣-na-ku [imKIŠIB] ⸢MU-MEŠ⸣

(r 5) In the sealing of that sealed tablet:

(witnesses)
r 66

⸢IGI⸣ md60-BAša₂-an-nu [DUMU ša₂ ...]

(r 6) Before Anu-iqišanni, [son of ...]

r 77

[m]⸢d60-AD⸣-[GUR DUMU ša₂ ... A mMU-MEŠ]1

(r 7) Anu-ab-[utir, son of ... descendant of Šumati]

r 88

[md60-DIN-su] ⸢DUMU?⸣ [ša₂ ... A mkur-i]2

(r 8) [Anu-bullissu] son [of ... descendant of Kuri]

r 99

m⸢tat⸣-tan-nu DUMU ša₂ m⸢d60-EN-šu₂⸣-[nu ...]-⸢x A m⸣[...]

(r 9) Tattannu, son of Anu-belšu[nu, ...] descendant of [...]

r 1010

mMU-d60 u mdna-na-a-MU DUMU-MEŠ ša₂ mdINANA-⸢x⸣-[x ...]

(r 10) Iddin-Anu and Nanaya-iddin, sons of Ištar-[...]

r 1111

mlib-luṭ DUMU ša₂ md60-NUMUN-MU A md⸢30⸣-[TI-ER₂]

(r 11) Libluṭ, son of Anu-zer-iddin, descendant of Sin-[leqi-unninni]

r 1212

md60-DIN-su-E DUMU ša₂ mšul-lum A md30-[TI-ER₂]

(r 12) Anu-balassu-iqbi, son of Šullum, descendant of Sin-[leqi-unninni]

r 1313

md60-MUnu [DUMU] ⸢ša₂⸣ mni-din-tu₄-d60 A mlu-uš-[tam-mar-dIŠKUR]

(r 13) Anu-ittannu, son of Nidintu-Anu, descendant of Luš[tammar-Adad]

r 1414

m⸢MU⸣-d60 ⸢DUMU ša₂⸣ mla-ba-ši

(r 14) Iddin-Anu, son of Labaši

r 1515

⸢m⸣[d]⸢60-BAša₂-an⸣-[nu] DUMU ša₂ mki-din-d60

(r 15) Anu-iqišanni, son of Kidin-Anu

r 1616

⸢m⸣dna-na-a-⸢MU⸣ DUMU ša₂ m i!(GAN.)BA-a

(r 16) Nanaya-iddin, son of Iqiša

uninscribed gap

1Restoration follows Wallenfels (2001), pp. 232-235.

2Restoration follows Wallenfels (2001), pp. 232-235.

3Wallenfels (2001), pp. 222-223 notes that the reading of this line is uncertain, and that it could either be read as unqu (ring) or kunukku (seal).
